channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Elon Musk could become world's first trillionaire as SpaceX files for largest IPO ever
item.description: Elon Musk's sprawling space exploration company, which also owns Starlink and xAI, was valued at $US1.25 trillion ($1.75 trillion) earlier this year, meaning Musk could become the world's first trillionaire if the offering goes ahead at that valuation.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thorpe

media:group.media:description: SpaceX currently has deals for 28 planned National Security Space Launch missions.

item.title: Court hears former AFL footballer Nick Stevens likely to face prison term
item.description: A Victorian court hears former AFL player Nick Stevens was a blue-collar worker but committed a white-collar crime, ahead of his sentencing for defrauding six families in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Wade Stephens

media:group.media:description: Ex-AFL player Nick Stevens appeared in prison greens during a County Court hearing on Thursday.

item.title: Olympics Minister stands aside after electoral commission makes referral to police
item.description: Queensland's Olympics Minister Tim Mander has stood aside from cabinet after he was referred to police by the Australian Electoral Commission.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Tim Mander says he has told Premier David Crisafulli he is "standing aside" while the matter referred to the Australian Federal Police is "being sorted".

item.title: Albanese's hard sell as budget opponents line up
item.description: For a group of people who declared they were prepared to tackle a post-budget scare campaign, the government has looked anything but in control this week.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers have been on the post-budget sell. It hasn't been easy.

item.title: 'Will be a deadline': Wagga homeless community urged to take new offer
item.description: The NSW minister for homelessness says people will be offered additional support to move out of camps around Wagga Wagga, but that they will be forced to relocate eventually.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Pezet

media:group.media:description: Rose Jackson visited Wagga Wagga to speak with the emergency cross-agency taskforce.

item.title: Drug testing of athletes to expand as influencers fuel illicit peptides boom
item.description: The nation's anti-doping agency reveals its plans to expand drug-testing in sport in response to the soaring popularity of unregulated peptides in the broader community.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Willan

media:group.media:description: Professor Lara Malins says peptides have the potential to be powerful therapies but rigorous testing is needed to ensure they're safe and effective.

item.title: Man with 'very serious' criminal history jailed over motorcyclist's death
item.description: The man had a "very high" level of methamphetamine in his blood when he crashed into the back of Ian Welldon's motorbike while failing to stop at a red light.
item.dc:creator: Dan McCray

media:group.media:description: Allan Eveleigh will spend the next 10 months behind bars before being eligible for parole.

item.title: Blues fighting for an Origin dynasty a decade in the making
item.description: Ten years after playing in New South Wales's breakthrough victory over Queensland, Isabelle Kelly returns to the Gold Coast on the cusp of launching a Blues Origin dynasty.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Isabelle Kelly will captain New South Wales in next Thursday's Origin finale.

item.title: Support worker cut keys to NDIS participant's home without knowledge, inquiry hears
item.description: A raft of alarming evidence has been aired at a parliamentary hearing into the integrity of the NDIS, with calls for the government to strengthen whistleblower protections.
item.dc:creator: Evan Young

media:group.media:description: Concerns have been raised at a parliamentary hearing into the NDIS about how complaints are handled.

item.title: What you need to know about tomorrow's ACT public school strike
item.description: For the first time in 15 years, ACT public school staff are going on strike in a two-hour work stoppage that will see all public schools, including specialist schools and preschools, keep their doors closed until 10:30am on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: ACT public schools will be closed for two hours on Friday morning, resulting in a later start to the school day.

item.title: Public transport to remain free in Tasmania for an additional year
item.description: The Tasmanian government has committed $24 million in the state budget towards keeping buses and ferries free for one more year.
item.dc:creator: Monty Jacka

media:group.media:description: Premier Jeremy Rockliff says keeping public transport free was a "significant" but "worthy" investment.

item.title: Defence minister 'disappointed' by lawsuit over Darwin port ownership
item.description: Chinese-owned company Landbridge launched legal action earlier this year following a pledge by the Prime Minister to return the port to Australian hands. Defence Minister Richard Marles says the government will fight the suit and remained committed to finding a domestic buyer.
item.dc:creator: Olivana Lathouris

media:group.media:description: Richard Marles says he is disappointed Landbridge has decided to sue the federal government over Darwin port.

item.title: AFLW's 2026 fixture to feature women's and men's double-headers
item.description: A series of double-headers involving the women's and men's competitions will take place after the AFLW season begins in August.

media:group.media:description: The women's fixture features six matches in Victoria around the men's grand final.

item.title: Coles investigates 'weird scratching' Apple gift card scam complaint
item.description: Coles is looking into a Melbourne mother's complaint about faulty Apple gift cards worth $2,000.
item.dc:creator: Emilia Terzon

media:group.media:description: A Melbourne artist is blasting Coles and has gone viral on Instagram, about how she purchased $2,000 worth of Apple gift cards and found their redemption codes scratched out. Coles is now refunding her purchase.

item.title: Female AFL creators say vile comments 'just one example of what we deal with'
item.description: A video circulated across social media of a group of men laughing about how one of them would "beat the f***" out of a group of female content creators.
item.dc:creator: Marnie Vinall

media:group.media:description: The Jumper Punch is a Carlton fan podcast.

item.title: Perth almost created a City of South Park in 2014 — here's why it might again
item.description: In 2015 the Barnett government abandoned plans to merge Perth councils — but now the idea has reared its head again. Here's what you need to know.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Mayes

media:group.media:description: Dysfunction at the City of Perth has prompted talk of it merging with the nerby Town of Vincent.

item.title: Queensland inquiry wraps as submissions say child safety complaints were ignored
item.description: Final hearings have concluded at Queensland's inquiry into child safety, with a "lengthy" report expected to be delivered to the government on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: An inquiry into child safety in Queensland has heard carer concerns were dismissed.

item.title: How Australia's EV uptake compares with the rest of the world
item.description: High petrol prices are driving EV sales around the world, with the leading international authority predicting 30 per cent of all new cars sold this year will be electric.
item.dc:creator: James Purtill

media:group.media:description: Chinese manufacturers are driving growth in EV market share across South-East Asian and Latin American markets.

item.title: What's being cut in Tasmania's state budget?
item.description: Treasurer Eric Abetz wants to find $1.47 billion in "efficiencies" in Tasmania's state budget. This is what's on the chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Adam Langenberg

media:group.media:description: Where are the cuts in the Tasmanian state budget?

item.title: Fears CSIRO job cuts will damage Australia's climate change adaptability
item.description: Peak body Science and Technology Australia is concerned the CSIRO's plan to cut 92 jobs, predominantly from its environment and climate science division, will negatively affect Australia's ability to assess and adapt to climate change.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Gore

media:group.media:description: The CSIRO has confirmed it will slash 92 full-time jobs from its Environment Research Unit.

item.title: Government to slash 1,700 jobs as Abetz looks to rein in Tasmanian spending
item.description: The Tasmanian government will slash 1,700 jobs through natural attrition and negotiated voluntary redundancies, as Treasurer Eric Abetz unveils his second state budget.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Eric Abetz is attempting to rein in the state's debt.

item.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine gets green light to challenge China's dominance
item.description: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.
item.dc:creator: Charles Geary and Tom Robinson

media:group.media:description: Arafura Rare Earths has given the green light to its Nolans project, decades after it was first flagged.

item.title: Government doing 'everything in our power' to keep SA safe from notorious paedophile
item.description: The South Australian government has filed two applications to either ensure notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is indefinitely detained or the subject of an extended supervision order.
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Leckie

media:group.media:description: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

item.title: MND has been called 'the beast'. We still don't know what causes it
item.description: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: Rabbitohs player Jai Arrow revealed news of his motor neurone disease diagnosis on Wednesday.

item.title: After losing four relatives to dementia, woman shares family's story
item.description: Gwen Knox, from Western Australia, has written a play inspired by her family that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Jackson

media:group.media:description: The immersive play invites audience members to eat scones along with the cast.
